Nice Android confused-deputy bug in Signal’s website APK 👀  The update receiver was exported and would relaunch a caller-controlled nested Intent.  Omit EXTRA_STATUS, hit STATUS_PENDING_USER_ACTION, set signal.user_initiated=true, and bypass the notification path.  The nested Intent preserves FLAG_GRANT_READ_URI_PERMISSION, so Signal launches the attacker’s activity while granting read access to URIs exposed by PartProvider, even though the provider itself is exported=false.  Practical impact: a zero-permission local app could obtain delegated access to Signal’s private attachment provider, enumerate attachment IDs and recover decrypted attachment bytes.  Fixed in 8.4.2 by @signalapp w/o any CVE id listed so far.  Research by @GHSecurityLab  and @yarlob securitylab.github.com/advis…  👉Takeaway for Android reviews:  exported=false on a ContentProvider is not enough if another exported component can forward an attacker-controlled Intent while preserving FLAG_GRANT_READ_URI_PERMISSION.  The provider remains private, the application grants access for you 🔥

🚨   A newly disclosed Windows BitLocker 0‑day vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-50661, exposes encrypted devices to physical attacks that can completely undermine Microsoft’s disk encryption guarantees.  The Flaw stems from a protection mechanism failure in BitLocker’s device encryption workflow, allowing an unauthorized attacker with hands-on access to a system to circumvent BitLocker and access data stored on the system drive.  Administrators are urged to deploy the July 2026 security updates across all affected Windows 10, Windows 11, and Windows Server instances and verify that BitLocker remains enabled and correctly configured after patching.  #cybersecuritynews

【サイバーセキュリティ動向分析】  以下に、今日（および直近1〜2日）の主要トピックを厳選し、各々について背景・影響・対策を詳しくまとめました。グローバルな脆弱性対応ニュースと、日本国内で実際に事業・生活に影響が出ているインシデントを中心に取り上げています。  1. Microsoftが過去最多規模のパッチをリリース（Patch Tuesday、7月14日発表）  背景 Microsoftは7月14日、Windowsをはじめとする自社製品群に対し、過去最多となる約570〜622件の脆弱性を修正するセキュリティ更新プログラムを公開しました。この異例の多さの背景には、AIツールを活用した脆弱性発見の加速化が挙げられます。Microsoft自身も「AIがコード解析や脆弱性特定を高速化している」と指摘しており、攻撃者側も同様にAIを悪用して脆弱性を素早く発見・悪用する動きが活発化している状況を反映しています。 特に注目されるのは、すでに実環境で悪用が確認されている2件のゼロデイ脆弱性（Active Directory Federation ServicesおよびSharePoint Serverにおける権限昇格の欠陥）と、BitLockerのセキュリティバイパス脆弱性（物理アクセスで暗号化データを読み取られる可能性）です。  影響 未対応のままだと、リモートコード実行（RCE）や権限昇格によるシステム完全掌握、データ窃取のリスクが極めて高くなります。特にSharePointやActive Directoryは企業・組織で広く使われており、標的型攻撃の足がかりになりやすいです。過去最多規模のため、パッチ適用時のシステム不安定リスクも指摘されており、企業は慎重な対応を迫られています。AIによる脆弱性発見の加速は今後も続き、Patch Tuesdayの規模がさらに拡大する可能性があります。  対策 優先度最高でパッチを適用（特にゼロデイ関連のCVE）。 適用前に必ずバックアップを取得し、可能であればステージング環境でテスト。 大量パッチのため、数日様子を見てから本番適用を検討（不安定報告を監視）。 EDR（Endpoint Detection and Response）やSIEMで異常検知を強化し、未パッチ期間のリスクを低減。 長期的に、AIを活用した脆弱性管理ツールの導入や、ゼロトラストアーキテクチャへの移行を加速させる。  2. SonicWall SMA1000のゼロデイ脆弱性とブラウザ（Chrome/Firefox）のクリティカル更新  背景 SonicWallはSMA1000シリーズ（セキュアアクセスアプライアンス）で2件のゼロデイ脆弱性（CVE-2026-15409、CVE-2026-15410）を公表し、早急なパッチ適用を呼びかけています。これらはリモートコード実行を可能にする深刻な欠陥です。 同時に、Google Chrome 150およびMozilla Firefox 152のセキュリティ更新もリリースされ、複数のクリティカル脆弱性が修正されました。特にFirefoxでは公開 exploitコードが存在しており、攻撃のハードルが低い状態です。  影響 SonicWall SMAはリモートアクセスやVPN的な用途で企業・組織で広く使われており、ゼロデイ悪用によりネットワークの境界が容易に突破される恐れがあります。ブラウザの脆弱性は、悪意あるWebサイト経由でのコード実行や情報窃取につながりやすく、一般ユーザーから企業まで幅広い影響が想定されます。公開exploitが存在するものは、攻撃が急速に広がるリスクが高いです。  対策 SonicWall製品ユーザーは即座に公式パッチを適用。 すべてのブラウザを最新版に更新（自動更新を有効化）。 ブラウザ拡張機能の最小化と定期的な監査を実施。 Webフィルタリングやサンドボックス技術を併用し、未知の脅威をブロック。 ネットワークセグメンテーションを徹底し、1台の機器が侵害されても横移動を防ぐ。  3. ニチレイグループへの不正アクセスによる大規模システム障害（7月13日発生・公表）  背景 国内最大手の冷凍・冷蔵物流企業であるニチレイグループで、7月13日朝6時50分頃に第三者による不正アクセス（サイバー攻撃）が確認され、グループ共通の基幹システムに障害が発生しました。原因は外部からの不正侵入で、ランサムウェアの関与は現時点で明らかになっていませんが、業務システムが物理的に停止する事態に至りました。食品サプライチェーンのデジタル化が進む中、コールドチェーンという社会インフラが直接標的になった典型例です。  影響 ニチレイロジグループの冷蔵倉庫における入出庫業務と、ニチレイフーズの冷凍食品出荷業務が停止。影響は取引先にも波及し、KFC（日本ケンタッキー・フライドチキン）全店舗への食材納品に遅延・欠品の可能性が生じています。他にもイオンやくら寿司など複数の小売・外食チェーンに影響が広がっており、冷凍食品の安定供給に支障をきたしています。 個人情報や顧客データの外部流出は現時点で確認されていませんが、復旧時期は未定で、事業継続計画（BCP）の脆弱性が露呈しました。食品という生活必需品の物流が止まることで、消費者への間接的な影響（品薄や価格変動）も懸念されます。  対策 企業側：サプライチェーン全体でのサイバーリスク評価を強化。多重化（複数物流事業者との契約）、手動バックアッププロセスの整備、オフライン/エアギャップバックアップの徹底。 ネットワークのセグメンテーションとゼロトラストの実装、EDR/SIEMによる24時間監視。 定期的なインシデント対応訓練（ tabletop exercise ）と、第三者によるペネトレーションテストの実施。 政府・業界：重要インフラ（食品物流）に対するサイバーセキュリティガイドラインの強化と、情報共有の活性化。 一般企業は自社のサプライチェーン依存度を再確認し、代替調達ルートを確保しておく。  4. 米ホワイトハウスがAI活用のサイバーセキュリティクリアリングハウス「GOLD EAGLE」を正式立ち上げ  背景 6月にトランプ大統領が署名したフロンティアAIモデルに関する大統領令に基づき、7月14〜15日頃に「GOLD EAGLE」と呼ばれるAIを活用したサイバーセキュリティ脆弱性調整機関が正式に発足しました。AIの急速な進化に伴い、AIモデル自体がサイバー攻撃の標的になったり、攻撃ツールとして悪用されたりするリスクが高まっていることを背景としています。  影響 公的機関と民間企業が脆弱性情報をAIで効率的に共有・分析できるようになり、AI関連の脅威に対する対応速度が向上すると期待されます。一方で、AIを活用した攻撃の高度化（自律型ランサムウェアなど）も同時に進行しており、防御側もAI抜きでは追いつかない状況が鮮明になっています。この取り組みは米国のAIイノベーションを安全に加速させる狙いがありますが、国際的な協力の在り方やプライバシーとのバランスも今後の焦点となります。  対策 AIを開発・利用する企業は、モデルセキュリティ（プロンプトインジェクション対策、モデル盗難防止など）を優先課題に。 脅威インテリジェンスの共有プラットフォームへの積極参加。 AIガバナンス体制を整備し、セキュリティを「後付け」ではなく開発プロセスに組み込む（Secure by Design）。 政府・企業ともに、AIの攻撃・防御両面での人材育成を急ぐ。 全体の傾向と追加のポイント AIの二面性が顕著：脆弱性発見の加速（防御側有利）と攻撃の高度化（攻撃側有利）が同時に進行。 サプライチェーン攻撃の増加：ニチレイのような事例は今後も増える可能性が高く、単一企業だけでなく「つながった社会全体」の強靭化が急務。 パッチ管理の重要性再確認：ゼロデイや公開exploitが存在する脆弱性は特に優先度を上げて対応を。 一般的な推奨対策（全組織・個人向け） パッチとソフトウェア更新を最優先（自動化推奨）。 多要素認証（MFA）の徹底とパスワードマネージャーの活用。 定期的なバックアップ（3-2-1ルール）と復旧テスト。 従業員教育の継続（フィッシング・ソーシャルエンジニアリング対策）。 インシデント対応計画の策定・訓練と、保険（サイバーリスク保険）の検討。 ゼロトラストアーキテクチャへの段階的移行。 これらのニュースは、サイバー脅威が「技術的問題」から「事業継続・社会インフラの問題」へとシフトしていることを強く示しています。早めの対応が被害を最小限に抑える鍵となります。状況は日々変化しますので、公式発表や信頼できるセキュリティ情報を継続的に確認してください。

【脆弱性管理】Microsoftが過去最大622件のCVEを一斉修正、SharePointとAD FSの悪用済みゼロデイ2件が最優先対応  2026年7月のPatch Tuesdayで、Microsoftは自社CVE数として過去最多となる622件を修正した。うち2件は既に悪用が確認されているゼロデイで、いずれも権限昇格の脆弱性である。オンプレミスSharePoint Serverの脆弱性(CVE-2026-56164)は未認証のリモート攻撃者による権限昇格を許し、MandiantのインシデントレスポンスチームとGoogle FLAREが実際の攻撃の中で発見・報告した。Active Directory Federation Services(AD FS)の脆弱性(CVE-2026-56155)は認証済み攻撃者による権限昇格を許し、Microsoft自身のDARTチームが発見している。いずれもCVSSスコア自体は控えめだが、片方は社内文書基盤、もう片方は組織全体の認証を署名するサーバーを狙う点で重大度以上の注意が必要である。両脆弱性ともCISAの既知悪用脆弱性(KEV)カタログには本稿執筆時点で未掲載であり、KEV登録を待たず即座のパッチ適用が推奨される。  今回の更新ではKerberosのRC4廃止作業も完了し、これまで管理者が使えていたロールバック用スイッチが削除された。今後RC4を明示的に許可していないサービスアカウントは、更新適用直後に認証エラーを起こす可能性があるため、事前にRC4監査イベントで対象アカウントを洗い出し、パスワードローテーションでAES鍵を生成させてからパッチを適用する順序が重要である。  Microsoftは7月9日の発表で、AIによる脆弱性発見の高速化に伴い今後も更新件数の増加が続くと予告しており、実際に自社の多モデルエージェント型スキャンシステムMDASHが5月には16件のバグを単独で発見した実績がある。攻撃者側もパッチ差分解析によるエクスプロイト構築を高速化させており、CVSSスコア順ではなく実際の悪用状況(KEV、EPSS等)に基づく優先順位付けの重要性が増している。 thehackernews.com/2026/07/mi…

🇮🇷🇺🇸 An IRGC-linked cyberattack disabled an American water system in 2023. The war's defenders say that's when this conflict actually began  Miad Maleki of FDD, born and raised in Iran, former Treasury official and Air Force officer, is the rare guest on this show who argues the campaign was unavoidable.  His threat matrix: the Pennsylvania water-system attack, speedboats and drone manufacturing shipped to Venezuela per Treasury designations he worked on himself, and Iran's proxy doctrine rebuilt in America's hemisphere, Hezbollah's playbook run out of Caracas.  He faced hard pushback on the 40,000 figure for the pre-war crackdown.   Maleki cited Human Rights Watch and briefed officials, then made the more striking concession: even at 5,000 the scale stands, body bags stacked on trucks across 31 provinces during an internet blackout.  His most surprising admission came on the bomb itself.  He long doubted Tehran wanted one, a threshold program that blackmails the world serves the regime better than a warhead, which is exactly why he argues no inspection regime can be trusted.  And his endgame flips every assumption: the most punishing outcome isn't occupation.  It's peace, sending the regime home to govern the wreckage it built.  The counterpunch that closed the show: we beat ISIS by handing Syria to a former ISIS commander.  @miadmaleki @WeTheBrandon

Los actores APT cada vez dependen menos de infraestructura propia para sus servidores de Command and Control (C2). En su lugar, abusan de plataformas legítimas como Telegram, utilizando su API o bots para establecer canales de comunicación cifrados que se mezclan con el tráfico normal y pueden evadir controles tradicionales de firewall y listas de reputación.  Los enlaces hacia el C2 pueden ocultarse en la descripción de canales, grupos o perfiles, e incluso dentro de conversaciones de bots privados, dificultando su descubrimiento durante tareas de OSINT o análisis de infraestructura. En escenarios más sofisticados, el primer enlace consultado por el malware puede apuntar a un grupo público aparentemente legítimo, utilizado únicamente como punto intermedio (dead drop resolver). Desde ese grupo, el malware obtiene un segundo enlace alojado en la descripción, un mensaje fijado o una conversación del bot, donde se encuentra la ubicación real del C2. Como resultado, las primeras conexiones observadas durante el análisis apuntan a un grupo completamente ajeno al atacante, dejando como rastro un recurso aparentemente inocente y desviando la atención de la infraestructura real.  El objetivo no es que Telegram aloje el malware, sino utilizarlo como puente de comunicación entre el implante y el operador, aprovechando una plataforma ampliamente permitida en entornos corporativos. Esta técnica está alineada con MITRE ATT&CK T1102.002 – Bidirectional Communication, donde se utilizan servicios web legítimos para el intercambio de comandos y la exfiltración de información.  ic3.gov/CSA/2026/260320.pdf

Encontramos os seguintes comandos no DC01 ( Imagem 1 ) e vou explicar o que cada um deles significa  - > lsadump::dcsync /domain:northwind.local /user:krbtgt :   Esse é um comando do mimikatz, o DCSync abusa do protocolo de replicação do Active Directory: o atacante finge ser outro Domain Controller e pede ao DC01 pra "replicar" as credenciais sem tocar no disco, sem dump de LSASS, pedir especificamente o usuário krbtgt é o objetivo mais valioso do AD: com o hash do krbtgt, ele forja Golden Tickets e vira o dono do domínio indefinidamente, mesmo depois de você "limpar" tudo.   MITRE ATT&CK: T1003.006 (DCSync)  -> vssadmin create shadow /for=C:  :   Cria uma Volume Shadow Copy (cópia-sombra) do disco C:. Por quê? O arquivo mais importante do DC, o NTDS.dit, o banco de dados que contém todas as contas e hashes do domínio,  fica travado pelo sistema enquanto o AD roda, você não consegue copiá-lo direto. A shadow copy é uma foto congelada do volume de onde dá pra extrair o arquivo travado. É o passo preparatório do próximo comando.   MITRE ATT&CK: T1006 / T1003.003  -> ntdsutil "ac i ntds" "ifm" "create full C:\Windows\Temp\ifm" q q  :   O ntdsutil é uma ferramenta legítima de administração do AD (mais um LOLBin), aqui abusada, o modo IFM (Install From Media) foi feito pra criar mídia de instalação de novos DCs, mas de quebra ele exporta uma cópia completa do NTDS.dit + a hive SYSTEM (que tem a chave pra descriptografar os hashes) pra C:\Windows\Temp\ifm Traduzindo: o atacante acabou de extrair o banco de credenciais inteiro do domínio  MITRE ATT&CK: T1003.003 (NTDS)  7z a -pInfected C:\Windows\Temp\ifm.7z C:\Windows\Temp\ifm  Usa o 7-Zip pra compactar a pasta ifm (com o NTDS.dit dentro) num arquivo .7z protegido por senha (-pInfected "Infected" é a senha, uma convenção comum em amostras de malware). Dois objetivos: reduzir o tamanho pra exfiltrar mais rápido e encriptar pra escapar de inspeção de conteúdo (DLP) na saída. MITRE: T1560.001 (Archive via Utility). Logo depois disso, na sua cadeia, vem a conexão de saída do DC01 pro IP do atacante na porta 443 a exfiltração de fato (T1041).

#threatreport #LowCompleteness Threat Intelligence Report: The Pro-Iran Hacktivist Ecosystem 2026 | 09-07-2026 Source: dti.domaintools.com/research… Key details below ↓  🧑‍💻Actors/Campaigns: Ddos-for-hire Cyber_islamic_resistance Islamic_cyber_resistance (🧠motivation: propaganda) Fatimiyoun (🧠motivation: sabotage) Dark_storm_team (🧠motivation: hacktivism, propaganda) Handala-hacking-team (🧠motivation: hacktivism, propaganda) Keymous Dienet Russian_legion (🧠motivation: hacktivism, propaganda) Killnet (🧠motivation: hacktivism, propaganda) Cyber_fattah_team (🧠motivation: hacktivism, propaganda) Rippersec (🧠motivation: hacktivism, propaganda) Cyb3r_drag0nz (🧠motivation: propaganda) Evil_markhors Nation_of_saviors (🧠motivation: propaganda) Hider_nex Noname057 (🧠motivation: hacktivism) Serverkillers  💀Threats: Credential_harvesting_technique, Password_spray_technique, Ddosia_botnet,  🎯Victims: Government, Telecommunications, Healthcare, Finance, Logistics, Open source infrastructure, Critical sectors, Israeli infrastructure, Western infrastructure, Public sector, ...  🏭Industry: Critical_infrastructure, Financial, Healthcare, Logistic, Government, Telco  🌐Geo: Russian, Iraq, Iranian, Israel, Middle east, Israeli, Afghan, Iran, United states, Kuwait, Malaysia  📚TTPs: ⚔️Tactics: 3 🛠️Technics: 0  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1078, T1190, T1485, T1491.002, T1498, T1588.002  💽Software: Telegram, Ubuntu  💻Platforms: cross-platform  #threatreport: The decentralized cyber ecosystem aligned with Iran, primarily operating in response to the U.S.-Iranian conflict, includes a diverse array of hacktivist groups, ideological militants, and influence networks. This loose coalition engages in activities such as DDoS attacks, website defacements, and hack-and-leak operations, notably facilitated through Telegram channels and dedicated websites. Attack claims emerge rapidly, providing actors plausible deniability and differentiating them from formal state entities while amplifying their impact during crises.  The technical capabilities displayed by these groups remain predominantly unsophisticated, with a focus on DDoS campaigns, propaganda, and extortion-style messaging rather than advanced intrusions. Their primary objective is not displayed capability but the ability to generate psychological and political pressure through coordinated efforts that leverage visibility and ideological narratives. Groups such as 313 Team exemplify this approach, performing high-profile DDoS attacks, such as targeted disruptions against Canonical and Ubuntu infrastructure, utilizing commercial DDoS-for-hire services rather than custom malware.  Other actors within the ecosystem include Handala Hack Team, known for its more sophisticated hack-and-leak strategies; Cyber Fattah Team, which engages in disruptive propaganda; and Fatimiyoun Cyber Team, focusing on critical infrastructure threats. Each group contributes to an overarching narrative of coalition-based cyber warfare, where even low-level operations can achieve significant media and psychological impact.  The threat landscape is characterized by a range of capabilities, from moderate to high risk for DDoS activities, disruption operations, and propaganda amplification. While some groups, like Dark Storm Team, blend traditional ideological objectives with criminal methodologies such as ransomware, others, like APT Iran, operate more as branding efforts without evident sophisticated operational skills.  Notably, groups such as Evil Markhors and Conquerors Electronic Army focus on credential harvesting and DDoS activities, contributing to the coalition's operational effectiveness by enhancing the potential for larger-scale attacks. Similarly, opportunistic actors like NoName057(16) and Killnet demonstrate increasing convergence of anti-Western narratives with the broader Iranian-aligned cyber environment, facilitating participation in coordinated disruption campaigns despite their varied ideologies.  In essence, the pro-Iran cyber ecosystem represents an evolution towards decentralized digital proxy warfare, prioritizing rapid disruption and psychological warfare over complex technical operations. Defensive strategies against this ecosystem center around enhancing DDoS readiness, employing web application firewalls, enforcing multi-factor authentication, and preparing for the reputational risks associated with inflated breach claims and real-time responses. Success hinges not just on thwarting intrusions but managing disruption and mitigating alert fatigue stemming from these coordinated, high-volume cyber activities.

sample_tweets.text: SAVE THIS POST.  101 QUESTIONS EVERY CHRISTIAN PARENT SHOULD ASK THEIR CHILD BEFORE THEY GROW UP  One day, someone will ask your child a question you are not in the room to answer.  Why do you believe in God?  Has science not made God unnecessary?  If God is good, why is there evil?  Why should the Bible decide what is true?  Why forgive someone who is not sorry?  Why love an enemy who hates you?  Why did Jesus have to die?  Are you afraid of death?  What is your life actually for?  And one day, the person asking these questions may not be a teacher, an atheist, a friend, or a stranger.  It may be life itself.  A death.  A betrayal.  An unanswered prayer.  A scientific discovery.  A political crisis.  A moral temptation.  A painful failure.  Or unexpected success.  And in that moment...  Mummy and Daddy may not be in the room.  Perhaps we know our children's shoe sizes, favourite foods, school subjects, and career dreams...  but have never really discovered the world being built inside their minds.  So talk to your children.  Not only preach at them.  Not only take them to church.  Ask them questions.  And when their answers frighten you, don't immediately silence them.  If your child says, “Loving your enemies sounds foolish,” ask why.  If they say, “I don't understand why God allowed this,” listen.  If they say, “Sometimes I'm afraid of dying,” don't shame them.  If they say, “I'm not sure I believe that,” resist the temptation to panic.  Truth is not so fragile that it must be protected from the sincere questions of a child.  Here are 101 conversations I believe Christian parents should have with their children before they grow up.  Not as an examination.  Not to manufacture perfect “Christian answers.”  But to discover what they truly think, what they secretly fear, what they have merely memorized, and what they genuinely believe.  HERE'S THE LIST:  1. Who is God to you, not just what have you been taught about Him?  2. If someone asked why you believe God exists, what would you say?  3. What do you think it truly means to know God?  4. What does loving God with all your heart, soul, and mind mean to you?  5. Is there anything about God you find difficult to understand or accept?  6. If you could ask God one completely honest question, what would it be?  7. What do you understand by the Gospel?  8. If a friend asked you to explain the Gospel without using “church words,” what would you say?  9. What do you understand by salvation, and why do you think it is necessary?  10. In your opinion, what is humanity's greatest problem?  11. Can education, money, technology, or good government completely solve the deepest problem of the human heart? Why?  12. Why do you think Jesus came into the world?  13. Why did Jesus have to die?  14. What does the resurrection of Jesus mean to you?  15. What is the difference between being a good person and being saved?  16. What do grace and repentance mean to you?  17. Can someone know many Bible verses, attend church, and still not truly know Christ?  18. How would you know whether Christianity is truly your faith and not simply your parents' religion?  19. Why do you personally believe in Jesus?  20. Is there anything about Christianity you secretly struggle to believe?  21. Do you believe the Bible should be the final arbiter of truth for a Christian? Why?  22. What should you do when your feelings tell you one thing and Scripture appears to say another?  23. Can something feel right and still be wrong?  24. Does something become true because most people believe it?  25. How can we distinguish between “the Bible says this” and “this is how I have interpreted the Bible”?  26. What should you do when sincere Christians disagree about a Bible passage?  27. If Scripture showed that something you strongly believed was wrong, would you be willing to change your mind?  28. Is asking difficult questions about God or the Bible a sign of unbelief, or can honest questions sometimes lead us deeper into truth?  29. Do you believe God created the universe? Why?  30. When you look at the stars, planets, oceans, forests, animals, and biodiversity, what do they make you think about?  31. Do you think life and the universe are ultimately products of mere chance, or do you believe there is purpose behind existence? Why?  32. What do you understand about the Big Bang and evolution?  33. Do you think science and belief in God must be enemies?  34. If science explains how a natural process happens, does that automatically mean God has nothing to do with it?  35. What kinds of questions can science answer very well, and what kinds of questions may require philosophy or theology?  36. Can a person be a serious scientist and a sincere Christian?  37. If scientific evidence appears to conflict with the way you understand a Bible passage, what should you do?  38. Do you think studying nature can help us appreciate God's creation more deeply?  39. If human beings are made in the image of God, how should that change the way we treat every human life?  40. If God is Creator, what responsibility do we have toward animals, forests, oceans, and the earth?  41. If God is good, why do you think evil exists?  42. Who or what do you believe is responsible for evil?  43. Should we blame Satan for every evil choice human beings willingly make?  44. Why do people sometimes knowingly choose things that hurt other people?  45. Why do you think innocent people sometimes suffer?  46. What would you say to someone who asks, “Where was God when this terrible thing happened?”  47. Can God bring good out of evil without calling the evil itself good?  48. Do you think we will understand every painful thing that happens to us in this life?  49. How should a Christian respond when God seems silent?  50. How do we resist evil without allowing hatred to turn us into the very thing we oppose?  51. What does it mean to overcome evil with good?  52. What is the difference between justice and revenge?  53. Can you fight injustice without hating or dehumanizing the person doing wrong?  54. Why did Jesus command us to love our enemies?  55. Does loving your enemies sometimes sound foolish or unfair to you? Why?  56. Why should we forgive people who may not deserve our forgiveness?  57. Does forgiveness mean pretending that nothing happened?  58. Can you forgive someone and still establish wise boundaries?  59. Have you ever tried to sincerely pray for someone who hurt you? What was it like?  60. How does the fact that Christ loved us while we were sinners change the way we look at people who wrong us?  61. Which requires more strength: taking revenge or refusing to let hatred control your heart?  62. What do you think death is?  63. Are you afraid of dying? Why or why not?  64. How do you think a Christian should view death, with fear, faith, sorrow, hope, or boldness?  65. Can a Christian grieve deeply and still have faith?  66. Why did Jesus weep at Lazarus' tomb even though He knew Lazarus would rise?  67. What do you think happens after death?  68. What does the resurrection of Christ teach us about the grave?  69. Why does the Bible sometimes describe death as an enemy, yet Christians can face it with hope?  70. If you knew you had only one year left to live, what would suddenly become important to you?  71. What do you think makes a life well lived?  72. Can someone become rich, famous, and successful and still waste their life?  73. What do you think your life is for?  74. Why do you think God allowed you to be born in this particular generation?  75. What gifts or abilities do you think God has placed in you?  76. Are your gifts only for your own success, comfort, and wealth?  77. What problem in the world makes your heart ache or makes you want to do something?  78. How could your education, career, creativity, or skills make another person's life better?  79. When people remember you someday, what do you hope they remember about the kind of person you were?  80. Would you rather be impressive before people or faithful before God? Why?  81. What does it mean for a Christian to be salt and light in the world?  82. How should Christians positively influence their schools, workplaces, communities, and nations?  83. Is ministry only what happens behind a pulpit?  84. Can being a scientist, teacher, doctor, engineer, artist, business owner, farmer, or politician become an avenue for serving God and humanity?  85. Should Christians care about poverty, injustice, education, healthcare, the environment, and how governments treat people? Why?  86. Do you think it is important for Christians to understand politics and public life?  87. Can a political party ever become an idol to a Christian?  88. If a politician you support does something wrong, should your loyalty prevent you from calling it wrong?  89. Can Christians disagree politically and still genuinely love one another?  90. What should happen when loyalty to a political group begins to compete with loyalty to truth and Christ?  91. Who are your closest friends, and how are they influencing the person you are becoming?  92. What kind of friend do you want to be when your friend is wrong?  93. What should you do if everyone around you is doing something you believe is wrong?  94. Have you ever been embarrassed or afraid to admit that you are a Christian?  95. What would you do if following Jesus cost you a friendship, popularity, or an opportunity you deeply wanted?  96. What is something you worry about but rarely tell anyone?  97. When you make a mistake, what are you most afraid I will do as your parent?  98. Is there anything you feel you cannot tell me? What can I do to become a safer and better listener?  99. What is one thing about our family you hope never changes, and one thing you pray we do better?  100. If you become a parent someday, what truth about God, life, and character would you desperately want your children to understand?  101. If, many years from now, you stood before God and looked back over the one life you were given, what kind of life would you be grateful to have lived?  Parents, please don't ask all 101 in a day. 😄  This is not an examination.  Pick one.  At dinner.  On the school run.  Before bed.  After church.  After a science lesson.  After someone dies.  After your child has been hurt.  Then ask the most important follow-up question:  “Why do you think that?”  And listen.  Your child may know the word salvation but not understand the Gospel.  They may say “God created the world” while secretly believing science has disproved God.  They may recite “love your enemies” while carrying a wound they have never told you about.  They may be terrified of death but ashamed to admit it.  They may know all the right Christian answers because they have already learned what makes Christian adults smile.  But what do they actually believe?  One day, our children will walk into rooms where we cannot follow them.  May we help them develop hearts that love God, minds that pursue truth, consciences shaped by Scripture, courage to stand alone, compassion for humanity, and a faith sincerely rooted in Jesus Christ.  ASK ONE QUESTION TONIGHT.  Then resist the temptation to preach for the first five minutes. 😄  Listen.  The most important spiritual conversation you ever have with your child may begin with a question.

This Week in Foreign Interference: 94 incidents logged. The volume matters less than the pattern. The institutions meant to detect and respond to foreign interference are being hollowed out at the same moment adversaries are escalating. That combination is not a coincidence.  The highlights:  Italy, July 9. Two Russian diplomats expelled after one of them reportedly burned the identities of Italian counterintelligence officers monitoring the Russian embassy. Not a run-of-the-mill expulsion. That's a penetration.  Brazil, July 8. A Russian SVR illegal ordered deported after spending years building false identities to infiltrate organizations in the U.S. and Netherlands. Classic Directorate S methodology. The question, as always: what did he move before anyone noticed.  Taiwan, July 10. A DPP staffer named Huang sentenced to ten years for espionage. He was running CCP agents inside the ruling party and successfully turned one of two targets. The court treated it seriously. The verdict should be read as a marker of how aggressively Beijing is trying to own Taiwan's political infrastructure from the inside.  Italy's expulsions and Brazil's deportation on the same week as Russia's prankster operation against the OSCE Secretary-General, July 10. Sinirlioglu, posing as Armenian PM Pashinyan, was recorded offering to help remove the Armenian church's chief bishop. The Vovan and Lexus operation is well-documented at this point. It keeps working anyway.  U.S. election security architecture, July 10. Trump fired the remaining EAC members months before the midterms. Earlier this week a CIA internal survey documented analyst concern that political pressure is distorting intelligence assessments. The GEC is already gone. FISA collection tools have lapsed. France tripled AI disinformation penalties this week. The U.S. is moving in the opposite direction.  Iran's post-Khamenei information operation, July 11. RT and Iranian state media coordinated on martyrdom framing within hours of the death, converting it into a mobilization resource. American influencers Jackson Hinkle and Max Blumenthal attended the funeral and provided Western-voice cover for Iranian state media. FARA questions are the polite way to put it.  China, July 12. Beijing issued a security advisory claiming Anthropic's Claude has built-in IP exfiltration. This is a documented counter-move pattern: generate reciprocal accusations against U.S. AI products whenever American restrictions tighten on Chinese ones. It is not a coincidence this arrived the same week DeepSeek restrictions were being discussed.  Japan, July 10. The LDP is advancing a foreign interference law that would allow warrantless communications interception. Japan has historically lacked this authority entirely, which Chinese and Russian services have exploited for years. Civil liberties opposition is real. So is the gap.  Iceland's EU referendum warning came during the NATO Summit. The Foreign Minister flagged Russian interference risk explicitly. Binary referenda on high-stakes geopolitical questions are among the most vulnerable formats. Watch that one develop.  Beyond the highlights: a dozen more China-linked espionage and influence items this week, including Beijing's glorification of a physical attack on pro-Taiwan Japanese commentator Akio Yaita and the EU Commission's draft procurement exclusion framework targeting Chinese and Russian state-linked firms. North Korea's Lazarus Group added botnet and DLL injection capabilities in the latest Famous Chollima wave. Russia's Wagner-linked disinformation machine completed France's expulsion from Burkina Faso. The historical items in this week's log, Glenn Souther's 1986 defection, the 1996 FBI spy arrest, the 1983 Soviet West German election operation, are worth reading as baseline: the methods are older than the internet.  Next week: watch the Iceland referendum interference warnings, the UK crypto donation ban vote, and whether the CIA assessment controversy produces any institutional response before it quietly disappears.  foreigninterference.org #foreigninterference #DisinformationCampaigns #InfluenceOperations #TransnationalRepression #CyberEspionage

In February 2025, Bybit lost $1.5 billion in Ethereum the largest crypto heist ever recorded.   North Korea's Lazarus Group didn't crack any cryptography.   They compromised a developer's machine at Safe, Bybit's multisig platform, and altered the interface so a routine transfer looked normal.   Bybit's team signed what they saw on screen.   What they actually approved rerouted 401,000 ETH straight into the attackers' wallets. $160 million was laundered within 48 hours.  Bybit wasn't careless.   The hack exposed a deeper hole: sign and trust what you see can be faked, and once funds move on a public ledger, everyone including the thief's own tracking tools can watch exactly where they go, forever.  That's the problem Umbra was built to solve, and it's why I've spent the past week actually using it instead of just reading the deck.  ✓ What @UmbraPrivacy actually does  Umbra is a self-custodial, Solana native wallet with privacy built into the foundation, not bolted on as an afterthought.   You keep your own keys, Umbra never touches your seed phrase but the way your money moves changes completely.  There's a Shield feature that takes your visible, on-chain balance and routes it into a private state.   Once shielded, your holdings stop showing up as a neat public ledger entry that anyone can total up.  From there, shielded transfers use zero-knowledge proofs to hide the three things that usually give you away:   who sent it,   who received it,   and how much moved.   Umbra runs on Arcium's encrypted execution network, so computation happens on encrypted data directly.   Private swaps work the same way. You can trade without broadcasting your position size or your entry to every MEV bot and copy-trader watching the mempool.  ✓ The feature that actually got me  Here's the part I didn't expect to care about.   I've had the "what if someone forces me to unlock my wallet" thought before, sitting in traffic with my phone out, doing a transfer at an agent location.  Every crypto user I know has run that scenario in their head at least once and had no real answer for it.  Umbra has Distress Mode.  You can set up a secondary wallet view that shows a limited, decoy balance.   If you're ever pressured, a physical threat, someone leaning on you at an ATM, you unlock into the decoy, not your real funds.  That's the first time I've seen a wallet design for the moment things go wrong in the real world, not just the moment a hacker tries something on-chain.   Most security features in this space are audits and multisigs important, but abstract.  Distress Mode is the first one that felt like it was built for someone who actually has to walk home with their phone in their pocket.  ✓ Why this matters for people like us, specifically  In most of the markets I write for, crypto isn't a trading hobby. It's rent, it's stock for a small business, it's a way to get paid by a client abroad.   A fully public ledger means a landlord, a business rival, or a stranger at a bus stop can look up what you're worth.  That's a physical safety and negotiating-leverage problem, and it's exactly what Distress Mode is answering.  Umbra doesn't ask you to trade away speed or usability to get privacy, it still runs on Solana, so you keep sub-second finality and near-zero fees.  It also keeps a compliance layer (viewing keys, risk screening) so privacy doesn't mean opting out of every legitimate rail.  It's live now on web, iOS, Android, and as a browser extension, no waitlist, no phased rollout excuse.  Questions this piece answers  1. What happened in the Bybit hack, and what does it reveal about crypto "security"?  2. What is Umbra and how does shielding a balance work?  3. How do private transfers and swaps hide sender, recipient, and amount?  4. What is Distress Mode, and why did it become the standout feature?  5. Does using Umbra mean giving up Solana's speed or low fees?  @ownershipfm

Russia's FSB targeted a NATO member's civilian power grid in the middle of winter, with roughly 500,000 people in the potential blast radius. The attack failed. Today — seven months later — the EU and UK stopped hedging and said so on the record.  The formal attribution, published July 13, lands on FSB Centre 16: Russia's elite signals intelligence and cyber operations division, distinct from APT28 and APT29, and increasingly operational in a way that goes well beyond intelligence collection. The weapon was DynoWiper, a new destructive wiper designed specifically to sever communications between renewable energy hardware and power distribution operators. Poland's grid stayed up. The intent is the story.  DynoWiper isn't a departure — it's a refinement. The wiper lineage runs directly through Sandworm's CaddyWiper campaign against Ukrainian utilities in 2023 and back to WhisperGate at the start of Russia's 2022 invasion: disrupt ICS/SCADA communication layers, create cascading operational failures, erase your forensic trail on the way out. What's new here is the target selection. Renewable integration infrastructure — smart inverters, DERMS, SCADA-to-renewable interfaces — is newer stack, less hardened, and less monitored than legacy grid SCADA. FSB Centre 16 noticed. That's not a coincidence; it's a documented evolution in ICS targeting doctrine.  The six-month gap between Poland's January confirmation and today's formal attribution is not bureaucratic drift. It's the operational tempo of Five Eyes-adjacent attribution work: you don't publish until you've exhausted SIGINT collection value, confirmed the forensic chain, and pre-staged the diplomatic response package. Today's announcement came with sweeping EU and UK sanctions and a co-authored NCSC technical advisory published simultaneously. That's a coordinated release, not a press conference.  The NCSC advisory is the part that actually matters operationally. The diplomatic attribution is historically significant — the West publicly naming FSB Centre 16 as a destructive threat actor against NATO civilian infrastructure changes escalation calculus on both sides of that line. But the advisory is the directive. It updates Russia's documented tradecraft against energy infrastructure and carries urgent guidance for critical infrastructure operators across Europe. Any energy sector security team reading the wire today should be treating it as an action item, not background reading.  The MITRE mapping tells its own story: T0831 (Manipulation of Control — disrupting renewable-to-distribution communications), T0829 (Loss of View — DynoWiper consistent with destructive ICS payload class), T0838 (Inhibit Response Function — targeting the communication layer specifically to prevent operator intervention). The attack was designed to blind operators and hold them there. The grid held anyway, but the architecture of the attack shows exactly where Centre 16 is probing next.  For European critical infrastructure operators, the NCSC advisory is not optional reading. Review network segmentation between IT and OT environments now, specifically at renewable integration points. Deferred patch windows need to close. This is an active targeting environment. The formal attribution changes the political record; the advisory changes what you do Monday morning.  Seven months to say it out loud. The FSB noticed Tuesday. We are nothing if not consistent.  Source: The Register — Connor Jones, 15:39 UTC, July 13, 2026. FCDO attribution statement and NCSC Joint Technical Advisory both published today.

불개미라는 KOL은 험한 한국 가상자산 시장에서 등불같은 KOL이라는 생각이 듭니다. 러그가 판치는 세상에서 그래도 자신 만의 기준을 가지고 투자자들에게 좋은 투자 정보와 기회를 제공하기 때문이죠.   DSCVR 프로젝트에 대해서 2주 전에 안보이는 것이 보이기 시작한다는 주제로 쇼츠가 올라왔었고 그 정보를 바탕으로 글을 작성했던 기억이 있습니다.   주요한 내용은  1. 단순한 소셜을 넘어선 '앱 배포 플랫폼' 소셜 기반 배포 레이어: 피드 내에서 AI 에이전트, Web3 앱, 인터랙티브 콘텐츠가 실시간으로 작동  강력한 개발자 생태계: Canvas 프레임워크, API, SDK를 지원하여 외부 앱을 피드 안에 손쉽게 통합  새로운 소셜 패러다임: 크리에이터와 팬이 플랫폼을 '공동 소유'하는 Web3 가치를 지향  2. 탄탄한 투자 배경 (Backers) 총 조달 금액: $9M (약 120억 원 규모, Seed 라운드 / 2022-07-25)  리드 투자자: 크립토 업계의 거물 Polychain Capital  참여 VC: Shima Capital, Upfront Ventures, Tomahawk VC, Fyrfly Venture Partners, BDMI 등 탑티어 투자사 참여  한다는 내용이었습니다. 이번에는 우리가 많이 알고 있는 명탐정 코난을 모티브로 한 1분 내외의 쇼츠 영상이 올라왔는데   먼저, 동영상을 전체적으로 살펴봤을 때의 느낀 점과 주요한 내용 그리고 이것을 통해 어떠한 투자를 할 수 있을지에 대한 아이디어에 대해서 기술해보고자 합니다.   -줄거리- 잘 나가던 크립토 트레이더였떤 불개미는 러그풀, 해킹 등으로  자산을 잃고 진정한 알파를 찾기 위한 여정에 나선다. 그 때 DSCVR 박사님에 의해 DSCVR에 대해서 알게 되고 여러 온체인 커뮤니티의 실시간 모니터링이 가능한 AI tracker 기능, 관심있는 프로젝트나 주제를 입력하면 리포트를 제공하는 AI Discovery,  $DS 토큰을 스테이킹하면 사용할 수 있는 프리미엄 기능(더 깊이 있는 AI 분석, 더 빠른 알림이 가능)을 통해 알파를 찾게 되었다.   -느낀점- 먼저 불개미 커뮤니티에서 쇼츠가 계속 나오고 있는데 퀄리티가 점점 더 좋아지는 느낌입니다. AI 동영상 플랫폼의 퀄리티가 좋아져서 그럴 수도 있지만 시나리오를 보고 고민한 흔적이 느껴졌고 그래서 프로젝트 팀에서 굉장히 좋아하지 않을까 하는 생각이 들었습니다.   그리고 여러 장면 중에서 인상깊었던 건 첨부한 사진처럼 'Web3의 불룸버그 터미널' 이라는 것이었습니다. 불룸버그는 Web2에서 팩트체크 분야로 나름 알려진 언론인데 DSCVR이 불룸버그의 역할을 한다는 문구가 이 프로젝트의 성격을 규정짓는 좋은 문구가 아닐까 하는 생각이 들었습니다.   더불어서 DSCVR이 프로젝트나 주제를 입력하면 제공하는 리포트가 있다는 내용에서 Surf가 생각이 났습니다. 이건 사용할 때  어떤 식으로 비용을 제공하게 될지 그런 생각이 들었습니다.  -투자 계획-  먼저 AI Tracker와 AI Discovery의 기능을 통해 커뮤니티의 의견이 무엇보다도 중요하고 빠른 의사 결정이 필요한 밈코인 영역에서 적극적으로 사용하게 될 것 같습니다.   또한, 더 정확한 의견을 받기 위해서 DS 토큰을 스테이킹해서 사용할 거 같기는 합니다. 개인적으로는 어떤 양으로 스테이킹했을 때 정확도가 향상되는지 특정 갯수의 캡이 있는지 이런 궁금증이 들긴 합니다.   또한 사용할 때의 비용이 어느 정도 드는지 그런 궁금증도 있습니다.

🇪🇺 UK and EU impose first simultaneous cyber sanctions as Poland attack is attributed to the FSB   💂‍♀️ For the first time, London and Brussels imposed sanctions under their respective cyber regimes on the same day, and they did it while jointly attributing to the FSB a destructive attack on Polish energy infrastructure that failed to interrupt electricity generation or heat delivery but that the UK assessed could have cut power to about 500,000 people during winter. The July 13 designations sweep from GRU senior leadership to the operators of Lumma Stealer to the staff of the Rybar influence operation, addressing military intelligence, criminal malware services and information manipulation through a coordinated cyber and hybrid policy response.    🔓 For cybersecurity, data privacy, regulatory compliance and eDiscovery professionals, the action can translate diplomacy into compliance obligations for organizations within scope: new names for sanctions screening, new risk in ransomware negotiations, new arguments in insurance coverage disputes and new government records whose internal use may need to be documented and preserved when preservation duties arise.    👀 Watch three things next: the EU’s pending 21st sanctions package, the first enforcement actions built on these designations and the first coverage dispute to cite the coordinated attribution. Coordinated designation may be emerging as a recurring operating model in Europe, and playbooks written for a single list will age quickly.   📰 Read the complete article from ComplexDiscovery OÜ's cybersecurity beat at complexdiscovery.com/uk-and-….  #CyberSecurity #CyberSanctions #ThreatIntelligence #CriticalInfrastructure #EnergySecurity #RiskManagement #Geopolitics #Compliance

1/3: The World Is Losing Trust in America  Trust doesn’t disappear overnight. It drains.  In a 2026 Pew Global Survey, only 32 percent, on average, across 36 countries, said the United States takes other countries’ interests into account in its decisions. In Germany, that share fell from 60 percent in 2023 to 23 percent. In France, it was 10 percent, and in Sweden, it was just 8 percent. In Canada, the share of people who see the United States as a reliable partner fell from 83 percent in 2022 to 35 percent in 2026. (⁠Pew Research Center)  This doesn’t mean that Europeans have become anti-American or that they want Russian and Chinese hegemony.  It does mean that they are starting to prepare for a world in which America’s word has an expiration date until the next election.  For a great power, that’s a strategic loss that doesn’t immediately show up in the budget.  America could win another military contract and lose another ally at home.  It could force Europe to pay more and at the same time teach it to be more skeptical.  It could win the deal and lose the system.  Europe is finally waking up, but late  European outrage at Trump often sounds hypocritical.  For decades, European governments have lived comfortably under the American umbrella. They spent on social tranquility, subsidies, and domestic popularity, while assuming that in a real war, American planes, satellites, intelligence, and nuclear forces would solve the problem.  Europe has made peace a state of nature.  But peace is not climate. It is a political and military achievement that must be maintained.  In The Hague in 2025, NATO countries committed to spending 5 percent of their gross domestic product on defense and security-related spending by 2035. Of this, at least 3.5 percent should be for actual military capabilities, and up to 1.5 percent for infrastructure, resilience, cybersecurity, and other related activities. (⁠NATO)  The European Union has also launched the Readiness 2030 program, which should allow for up to 800 billion euros in additional defense investment by the end of the decade. Member states’ spending has already increased from 343 billion euros in 2024 to approximately 381 billion in 2025. (⁠Consilium)  This is necessary.  But money is not an army.  A budget is not a combat capability.  An order is not a delivered system.  And European strategic autonomy is not born from another summit with translators, flags and a common photo.  Europe needs common production, standardized ammunition, air defense, transport corridors, satellite intelligence, cyber defense and a clear command structure.  And above all, out of political will.  Because a continent that always wants someone else to die for its values ​​has no values. It has conveniences.  Three possible NATOs  The future of the Alliance could go in three directions.  The first is for NATO to remain an American protectorate, but at a higher price. Europe would pay more, buy mostly American weapons, and receive American protection while Washington continues to define strategic boundaries.  This is the easiest model. And the most humiliating.  The second option is a strong European pillar within NATO. Europe takes over the main conventional defense of its continent, develops industry and capabilities, but retains American nuclear deterrence, intelligence, and transatlantic ties.  This is the most reasonable model.  No NATO without America.  A NATO that does not depend on the character of an American president.  The third option is a gradual disintegration. Not necessarily through formal withdrawal. NATO could continue to exist as a building, a secretariat, meetings and declarations, but internally become a group of countries unsure whether the others will come to their aid.  Then the Alliance would not be destroyed by Russian tanks.  It would be emptied of mistrust.  The End of Atlantic Innocence  MAGA said something Europe had long refused to hear.  America will not carry the world on its back forever.  But behind this fair statement lies a much more dangerous idea: that any relationship is justified only if it brings immediate benefit. That an ally is a client. That trust is naivety. That a great power owes nothing to the order from which it itself has gained the most.  Here American individualism turns into national egoism.  Alexis de Tocqueville warned that individualism can confine a person to a narrow circle of family and friends, while it leaves the larger society to fend for itself. (⁠Marxists Internet Archive)  MAGA applies this logic to an entire country.  America withdraws into its own circle.  Guards its border.  Counts its money.  Measures the gratitude of its allies.  And it leaves the world to fend for itself, except when it can sell it weapons, gas, or defense.  But a great power does not cease to be an empire when it speaks out against globalism.  It simply becomes an empire without a sense of responsibility.  NATO will survive not if Europe continues to behave like an American minor, nor if America treats its allies as overdue debtors.  It will survive if both sides understand something old and simple.  Alliance is not love.  It is not charity.  But it is not a receipt either.  It is a promise made in peacetime that in the day of danger we will not first ask how much the other is worth.  Because when we start calculating the price of friendship at the moment of attack, we no longer have allies.  We have customers.  And the customer can always find another supplier.

Conference recordings from BlueHat IL 2026 are now online!   BlueHat IL 2026 conference page -> (microsoftrnd.co.il/bluehatil…)  Full conference agenda -> (microsoftrnd.co.il/bluehatil…)  Keynote video recordings -> (youtube.com/playlist?list=PL…)  We’ve highlighted a few talks worth checking out:  • Recovery Reimagined: Attacking and Securing Windows Recovery -> (youtube.com/watch?v=DkuEmspD…). The researchers present their study of Windows Recovery Environment and 11 new vulnerabilities that allow bypassing BitLocker and extracting data from encrypted drives.  • ShadowMQ: Leveraging AI Architecture for RCE Across Leading Inference Servers -> (youtube.com/watch?v=15xqr43o…). The speaker discusses remote code execution flaws in internal channels of popular AI inference servers, including projects from Meta, NVIDIA, Microsoft, vLLM, and SGLang.  • The Stream Is Dead, Long Live the Stream: How HTTP/2 Lets Dead Streams Keep Servers Working -> (youtube.com/watch?v=GdSopFCP…). The talk covers the MadeYouReset vulnerability in HTTP/2 that enables request floods and denial of service. The issue affected Tomcat, Netty, F5, and several other implementations.  • Exploiting AI Orchestration Zero-Days via PostgreSQL Internals -> (youtube.com/watch?v=71QAblR4…). The researchers disclose multiple 0-day vulnerabilities in Langflow, n8n, and Activepieces — including remote code execution, sandbox escape, session hijacking, and arbitrary file read via PostgreSQL internals.  • ChainLeak: From AI Framework to Cloud Secrets -> (youtube.com/watch?v=DYMKhDvq…). This talk examines two vulnerabilities in Chainlit that enable remote file theft, cloud key and secret exfiltration, SSRF exploitation, and eventual server compromise.  • Hybrid Cloud – A Novel Vulnerability Playground -> (youtube.com/watch?v=Cm8keeVj…). The presenters analyze four remote code execution and privilege escalation vulnerabilities in Azure Arc extensions that link on-prem systems with the cloud.  • VoidLink Internals Inside Chinese Commercial Grade Cloud Native Malware -> (youtube.com/watch?v=ZJeSwZuc…). The researchers dissect the VoidLink cloud-based malware framework, which integrates Linux kernel modules, eBPF, cloud credential theft, and over 30 components for offensive operations.  #dbugs_attacks

𝑭𝒊𝒓𝒔𝒕, 𝑰 𝒘𝒂𝒏𝒕 𝒕𝒐 𝒔𝒂𝒚 𝒂 𝒗𝒆𝒓𝒚 𝒃𝒊𝒈 𝒕𝒉𝒂𝒏𝒌 𝒚𝒐𝒖 𝒇𝒐𝒓 𝒂𝒍𝒍 𝒕𝒉𝒆 𝒃𝒊𝒓𝒕𝒉𝒅𝒂𝒚 𝒘𝒊𝒔𝒉𝒆𝒔 — 𝒘𝒆 𝒈𝒆𝒏𝒖𝒊𝒏𝒆𝒍𝒚 𝒂𝒑𝒑𝒓𝒆𝒄𝒊𝒂𝒕𝒆𝒅 𝒆𝒗𝒆𝒓𝒚 𝒔𝒊𝒏𝒈𝒍𝒆 𝒐𝒏𝒆. 🙏  Now our Cybersecurity Awareness Lessons   Day 11 ✅ #𝟑𝟎𝐃𝐚𝐲𝐬𝐂𝐲𝐛𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐞𝐜𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐲𝐀𝐰𝐚𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬  Today, we’re talking about malware — what it is, how it spreads, and how to protect yourself.  Most people think malware just means a slow computer.  Sometimes it does.  Sometimes it shuts down hospitals.  In May 2017, WannaCry infected over 200,000 computers across 150 countries in just a few days. Hospitals cancelled surgeries, staff lost access to patient records, and critical systems went offline.  The scary part?  A security patch already existed.  Many systems simply hadn’t been updated.  𝑀𝑎𝑙𝑤𝑎𝑟𝑒 𝑐𝑎𝑛 𝑎𝑟𝑟𝑖𝑣𝑒 𝑞𝑢𝑖𝑒𝑡𝑙𝑦 — 𝑡ℎ𝑟𝑜𝑢𝑔ℎ 𝑎𝑛 𝑒𝑚𝑎𝑖𝑙 𝑎𝑡𝑡𝑎𝑐ℎ𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡, 𝑎 𝑑𝑜𝑤𝑛𝑙𝑜𝑎𝑑𝑒𝑑 𝑓𝑖𝑙𝑒, 𝑜𝑟 𝑎 𝑤𝑒𝑏𝑠𝑖𝑡𝑒 𝑦𝑜𝑢 𝑑𝑖𝑑𝑛’𝑡 𝑡ℎ𝑖𝑛𝑘 𝑡𝑤𝑖𝑐𝑒 𝑎𝑏𝑜𝑢𝑡 𝑣𝑖𝑠𝑖𝑡𝑖𝑛𝑔.  The update took minutes. Recovery took months.  When was the last time you updated your device? Be honest.  #CyberSecurity #30DaysCybersecurityAwareness #D_CyberEngr #FromCircuitToCyber

Iran's Permanent Representative to the UN, Ambassador Amir-Saeid Iravani, sent a formal letter to Secretary-General António Guterres and the rotating UN Security Council president on July 14, rejecting US claims that Yemen's Houthi movement (Ansar Allah) acts on Iran's behalf as "completely baseless," "misleading, politically motivated, and without any evidence."  He stated Sanaa's authorities "represent a significant portion of the Yemeni people and make their decisions independently and in accordance with what they perceive to be the interests of the Yemeni people."  In the same letter, Iravani also rejected US claims of continued commitment to the Islamabad MOU as equally baseless, alleging Washington has committed 42 documented violations of the agreement since it was signed, and stated, "The United States is the aggressor, not the victim. It has committed gross violations of the United Nations Charter by launching two wars of aggression, along with the Israeli regime."   The letter came the same day the UN Security Council voted 13-0 (China, Russia abstaining) to extend Houthi Red Sea monitoring through January 2027.

A predator who posed as a taxi driver and picked up a vulnerable woman in Newcastle has been jailed – with help from dedicated officers intervening.  On Saturday, December 20, last year, a woman in her 20s had been enjoying a night out with friends in the Groat Market area of the city centre.  At around 2.20am the following day, she decided to book a taxi to head home but soon became separated from her friend when leaving the venue.  While looking for her driver outside, the woman was approached by a man she did not know, who spoke to her before picking her up from the floor and putting his arm around her.  The man – now known to be Kuldip Chahal – repeatedly directed and physically guided the woman through crowded streets for around ten minutes towards his white Volkswagen Golf parked on Fenkle Street.  The woman, who was alone at the time and intoxicated, was dragged and forced by the offender into his front passenger seat after being told he was taking her home.  However, Chahal was unaware he was already being observed as part of Operation Salus, which sees plain clothes and uniformed officers on duty to identify vulnerability in the night-time economy.  Working alongside officers monitoring city centre CCTV cameras in real time, the team on the ground swiftly stopped the vehicle before it could drive away.  The woman was immediately safeguarded after it was found she did not know Chahal by name, with quick-thinking officers arresting him on suspicion of kidnap.  Further enquiries by the investigating officer, working alongside neighbourhood policing colleagues, quickly established that Chahal was not a licensed taxi driver and had approached a number of other women in the city centre before targeting the victim-survivor.  The now 51-year-old, of Plessey Road, Blyth, was also captured on a trawl of CCTV footage physically picking up the victim from the ground, with the evidence gathered leading to him being charged with kidnap later that day.  Despite denying the offence, he was found guilty following a one-week trial at Newcastle Crown Court in May and remanded into custody.  Today (Thursday), he appeared before the same court where a judge handed him a seven-year prison term. He will also serve an extended licence period of three years.  Chahal was also made subject to a lifetime Sexual Harm Prevention Order (SHPO).

🔰Build like your reputation depends on every line of code. Because it does.  In Web3, anyone can copy your code. Anyone can fork(duplicate) your idea and spin up their own version in a matter of hours. But no one can copy your reputation. No one can fork the trust you have built with your community, your users, and your collaborators. That trust takes years to earn, but it can disappear in seconds with one bad decision, one overlooked vulnerability, or one moment of carelessness.  Think about it this way. When you write code, imagine you will have to stand in front of a room full of skeptical auditors and explain every single line. Would you feel confident? Would you be able to defend your choices? That is the standard you should hold yourself to every day. Not because someone is watching right now, but because someday someone will be.  When you build a project, act like you will be maintaining it for the next five years. Not just the next five weeks until launch. The decisions you make today, the shortcuts you take, the tests you skip, they all become someone else's nightmare later. And that someone might be you, trying to fix a broken contract at 2 AM while users are losing money.  👉The developers who last in Web3 are not always the smartest or the fastest. They are the ones people trust. They are the ones who communicate clearly when things go wrong. They are the ones who prioritize security over speed and transparency over hype. They understand that their name on a project is a promise, not just a byline.  What does trust mean to you in the Web3 space? Share your thoughts below.  #Web3 #BlockchainEthics #ReputationMatters #BuildWithIntegrity

France–Russia Shadow War: Five-Year Escalation - France and Russia are entering a sustained confrontation below the threshold of direct armed conflict. The Anna Novikova proceedings may become a symbolic accelerant, but the publicly available official record does not substantiate the claim that she is prosecuted solely for humanitarian deliveries. No accessible French judicial document independently confirms a hearing on Wednesday, 15 July 2026, the alleged denunciation, or the stated 15-year maximum sentence; these details must therefore remain unverified. France’s posture is broader than “media action”: it combines judicial enforcement, counter-intelligence, cyber defence, attribution and exposure of information operations. Russia’s probable response will be asymmetric rather than reciprocal: narrative warfare, diplomatic coercion, cyber intrusion, economic intelligence and proxy mobilisation. French defence, aerospace, energy, nuclear, transport, telecommunications and Ukrainian-support networks constitute the most attractive intelligence targets. The highest-probability 2026–2031 outcome is managed but intensifying hybrid confrontation, not direct Franco-Russian military conflict. The greatest strategic danger is cumulative escalation produced by ambiguous incidents whose sponsorship cannot be proved rapidly. France’s central vulnerability is not a lack of intelligence capability, but the political and legal tension between national security enforcement, evidentiary secrecy and civil-liberties legitimacy. - debuglies.com/2026/07/15/fra… via @https://debuglies.com

'일본 비트코인 ETF 승인'보다 '암호자산의 금융상품 전환'이 더 중요하다  1. 법적 지위의 변화가 구조다 ETF 상장 자체는 2027~2028년이라 아직 시간이 남았다. 하지만 '암호자산을 증권·채권과 동일한 금융상품으로 재분류'하는 법안이 중의원을 통과하고 참의원 위원회까지 패스했다는 건, 일본 금융 시스템이 암호자산을 '제도권 변방'에서 '정규 금융시장 구성요소'로 격상시켰다는 의미다. 이건 ETF 승인보다 훨씬 큰 일이다 — ETF는 이 재분류의 '응용'일 뿐이다.  2. 세금이 핵심 변수다 55%에서 20%로의 인하는 단순한 세율 변화가 아니다. 일본은 그동안 암호화폐 세금이 OECD 최고 수준이어서 일본 내 트레이더들은 싱가포르·두바이로 이탈하거나 거래 자체를 위축시켜왔다. 20% 평탄세는 증권과 동일한 과세 기준이다. 2028년 시행 목표이지만, 이 로드맵만으로도 일본 거래소에는 '제도적 확실성'이 생긴다.  3. 은행권 채널 개방 재분류의 결과로 일본의 메가뱅크(미쓰비시UFJ·미즈호·스미토모)가 암호자산 수탁·ETF 유통에 참여할 수 있는 법적 근거가 생긴다. 미국에서 골드만삭스·JP모건이 비트코인 ETF 수탁에 뛰어든 패턴과 동일한 흐름이다. 일본은행의 마이너스 금리 종료(2024) 이후 일본 금융권은 새로운 수익원이 절실한 상황이라는 점도 타이밍이 맞아떨어진다.  4. Mt.Gox의 아이러니 일본이 암호화폐 규제를 처음 만든 건 2014년 Mt.Gox 해킹 사태 이후였다. '범죄·투기 수단'으로 인식되던 자산이 12년 만에 '금융상품'이 됐다. 법적 프레임의 180도 전환이다. 이 변화의 속도는 한국에도 시사점이 있다 — 일본이 '먼저 규제를 명확히 하고 시장을 열었다'는 점에서.  관전 포인트는 ETF 승인 그 자체가 아니라, 2027년 제도 시행까지 일본 금융청(FSA)이 어떤 수탁 기준과 공시 의무를 설계하느냐다. 이 디테일에 따라 일본발 기관 자금의 유입 속도가 결정된다.

❓우리 회사는 인터넷에 얼마나 노출되어 있을까?  지난 7월 8일 진행된 ‘Criminal IP Q&A 웨비나’에서는 외부 공격표면 관리(ASM)와 외부 노출 자산 관리에 대해 보안 실무자들이 가장 궁금해하는 내용을 살펴봤습니다.  📊사전 설문으로 살펴본 보안 담당자들의 주요 관심사 가장 궁금해한 질문은 클라우드 자산 관리(46%), 가장 우려하는 외부 노출 위험은 관리자 로그인 페이지의 외부 노출(51%)로 나타났습니다. 한편 외부 노출 자산 점검을 취약점 진단이나 모의해킹 시에만 진행한다는 응답이 가장 높게 나타났습니다.  🔍공격표면 관리의 핵심 과제 관리에서 누락된 노출 자산은 보안망 공격의 경로가 됩니다. 기업 외부 관점에서 실제 인터넷 노출 자산을 지속적으로 확인하고 관리하는 것이 중요합니다. Today's Engineering Concept: '𝗥𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗟𝗶𝗺𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴'  𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝗥𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗟𝗶𝗺𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴? Rate limiting is the practice of restricting how many requests a user or system can make within a specific period.  𝗪𝗵𝘆 𝗱𝗼𝗲𝘀 𝗶𝘁 𝗺𝗮𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿? Without rate limiting, a single user or malicious bot could overwhelm your server, degrade performance, or abuse your APIs.  𝗥𝗲𝗮𝗹-𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗹𝗱 𝗲𝘅𝗮𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗲 Imagine a login endpoint with no rate limit. An attacker could attempt thousands of password combinations every minute. A simple rate limit can significantly reduce the effectiveness of brute-force attacks.  𝗛𝗼𝘄 𝗶𝘀 𝗶𝘁 𝗶𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗱? Most systems track requests by IP address, user account, or API key. Once a predefined limit is reached, the server temporarily rejects additional requests, often with an HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) response.  𝗪𝗵𝗲𝗿𝗲 𝗶𝘀 𝗶𝘁 𝘂𝘀𝗲𝗱? • 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: GitHub's REST API limits how many requests you can make per hour to prevent abuse and ensure fair usage for everyone.  • 𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗶𝗽𝗲: Every payment request can include an Idempotency-Key, ensuring a customer isn't charged twice if the same payment request is retried.  • 𝗢𝗽𝗲𝗻𝗔𝗜: The API enforces rate limits on requests and tokens per minute, helping maintain reliability and preventing a single application from overwhelming the service.  • 𝗫 (𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗲𝗿𝗹𝘆 𝗧𝘄𝗶𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿): X limits actions such as following many accounts, liking posts, posting, or sending DMs within a short period to reduce spam and bot activity.  • 𝗖𝗹𝗼𝘂𝗱𝗳𝗹𝗮𝗿𝗲: Cloudflare lets website owners configure rules like "block or challenge any IP that makes more than 100 requests in a minute" to protect against abuse and DDoS attacks.  ...and almost every public API uses rate limiting to protect its infrastructure, ensure fair usage, and maintain service availability.  𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝘁𝗮𝗸𝗲𝗮𝘄𝗮𝘆 A reliable system doesn't just answer requests. It also knows when to say "not now. It's too many from YOU."

Not every company is ready to talk about #resilience.  Some still operate in a purely reactive model—waiting for an incident to occur before taking action. Others understand that maximum availability is a powerful competitive differentiator.  #DDoS is no longer just a technical threat. It’s a financial, operational, and reputational risk.  If your current strategy relies on simply absorbing attacks rather than preventing impact, it’s time to evolve your architecture. Learn More: nexusguard.com/products

30일. 하사비스가 미국에 요구한 숫자  30일.  데미스 하사비스가 제시한 기간이다. 최전선 AI 모델을 세상에 내놓기 30일 전, 새로 만들 감독기구에 먼저 제출해서 검사를 받으라는 것이다.  제안한 사람의 위치가 이 제안의 무게를 결정한다. 하사비스는 구글 딥마인드의 CEO이자 알파폴드로 노벨 화학상을 받은 사람이고, 제미나이를 만드는 조직의 수장이다. 규제를 반길 이유가 가장 적은 자리에 있는 사람이, 자기 회사를 포함한 업계에 관문을 세우자고 말했다.  7월 14일, 그는 개인 명의의 에세이 「A Framework for Frontier AI and the Dawning of a New Age」를 공개했다. Axios와의 인터뷰에서는 시한까지 못박았다. 연내에 첫 발을 떼야 한다는 것이다.  무엇을 만들자는 것인가  모델은 월스트리트에서 가져왔다. FINRA — 미국 증권업계가 돈을 대서 만들고, 증권거래위원회(SEC)가 위에서 감독하는 자율규제기구다. 정부가 직접 감독관을 파견하는 방식도 아니고, 업계가 알아서 하겠다는 방식도 아니다. 그 사이에 있다.  AI판 FINRA는 이렇게 굴러간다.  기준선을 긋는다. 표준기구가 정한 벤치마크를 넘는 모델은 '프론티어급'으로 분류된다. 그런 모델을 가진 조직은 '프론티어 랩'이 된다. 회사 규모나 국적이 아니라 모델의 능력이 선을 정한다.  출시 전에 검사한다. 처음에는 자발적으로, 출시 30일 전까지 모델을 넘긴다. 검사 절차가 실효성을 입증하면 다음 단계로 넘어간다 — 통과하지 못한 프론티어 모델은 미국 시장에 배포할 수 없게 된다.  무엇을 보는가. 사이버 공격 능력, 생물학적 위협, 그리고 '기만(deception)' — 모델이 안전장치를 우회하려 드는지, 사람을 속이려 드는지를 본다. AI 생성 이미지의 워터마크, 사람이 읽을 수 있는 형태의 추론 과정 공개도 관행 목록에 들어간다.  누가 앉는가. 하사비스가 그린 이사회는 튜링상 수상자급 독립 기술 전문가가 과반이고, 여기에 산업계·정부·오픈소스 진영 대표가 함께 앉는다. 재원은 대부분 업계가 댄다 — 최고 수준의 인재와 대규모 테스트용 컴퓨팅을 사려면 돈이 필요하기 때문이다.  왜 지금이라고 말하는가  에세이에서 그가 가장 힘주어 쓴 문장은 위험 목록이 아니다. 속도의 불균형이다.  "최전선의 진보가 그 기술에 대한 우리의 이해를 앞지르고 있다."  상업적·지정학적 경쟁이 발전을 가속하는 건 사실이다. 그 대가로 아무도 다음에 무슨 일이 생길지 확신하지 못하는 상태가 됐고, 전문가들끼리도 의견이 갈린다. 불확실성이 크고 판돈이 클 때 합리적인 태도는 "신중한 낙관"이며, 지금은 그렇게 하고 있지 않다는 게 그의 진단이다.  Axios 인터뷰에서 그는 이미 벌어지고 있는 AI발 사이버 공격을 **"경고 사격"**이라고 불렀다. 아직은 예고편이라는 뜻이다.  그러면 다른 길은 없나  기업 자율 서약으로 안 되나? 이미 하고 있다. 국제 AI 안전 보고서에 따르면 최소 12개 AI 기업이 자체 '프론티어 안전 프레임워크'를 발표했고, 그 절반 이상이 2025년에 나왔다. 문제는 기준이 제각각이고, 통과했는지 여부를 검증할 제3자가 없다는 것이다.  정부가 직접 규제하면 되지 않나? 하사비스가 든 이유는 속도다. 벤치마크는 몇 달이면 포화된다. 그래서 그는 평가를 분기마다 갈아엎고, 낡은 시험지는 폐기하자고 쓴다. 법률 개정 주기로는 따라갈 수 없는 리듬이다.  작은 회사들은 어떡하나? 프론티어급이 아닌 모델 — 스타트업·학계의 모델은 이 절차에서 면제된다. 문턱을 넘는 능력을 가진 소수에게만 적용된다.  미국만 하면 중국은? 규칙 자체는 국적을 가리지 않는다. 오픈 모델이든 폐쇄 모델이든, 어느 나라에서 만들었든 프론티어급이면 대상이다. 다만 이를 강제할 지렛대는 하나뿐이다 — 미국 시장 접근권. 하사비스는 이 미국발 체계가 국제 공통 표준의 출발점이 되기를 기대한다고 적었다.  남는 것  이 제안에는 다이얼이 하나 더 달려 있다. 상황이 심각해지면 표준기구가 프론티어 랩들의 개발 속도를 함께 늦추는 조율까지 할 수 있게 하자는 조항이다. 경쟁의 브레이크를 업계 공동으로 잡자는 이야기다.  그리고 하사비스 자신이 이 설계의 약한 고리를 명시해뒀다. 초기 시험 문제는 프론티어 랩들과 상의해서 만든다. 그래서 그는 표준기구가 결국 랩과 무관한 자체 비공개 시험을 만들 역량을 갖춰야 한다고 못박는다. 시험 문제를 낸 사람이 시험을 보면, 모델은 그 시험에만 최적화되기 때문이다.  이 제안이 실제 제도가 될지는 지금 단계에서 반반이다. 자발적 단계까지는 업계 이해와 어긋나지 않지만, 의무화 단계에서 시장 접근권이 걸리는 순간 계산이 달라진다.   다만 다른 프론티어 랩들이 같은 방향으로 서명하기 시작한다면 이 그림은 빠르게 굳는다 — 반대로 자발적 제출 단계에서 참여가 저조하면, 이 프레임은 에세이로 남는다.  $GOOGL #AI규제 #딥마인드 #하사비스 #프론티어AI #AI거버넌스

<일일 뉴스> - 2026.07.14. *북한 연계 해킹조직 APT37, ‘한국 연구 분야’ 겨냥 사이버 공격  voakorea.com/a/nk-hacking-ap…  북한 정부의 지원을 받는 것으로 알려진 사이버 첩보조직 APT37. 한국의 연구·정책 분야 종사자들을 겨냥한 이들의 사이버 공격 정황이 포착되었다. 실제 행사 자료집을 악성코드와 함께 배포해  이용자가 감염 사실을 눈치채지 못하도록 한 것으로 분석되었다.  이러한 분석결과는, 한국의 사이버 보안업체 지니언스 시큐리티 센터의 보고서를 통해 공개되었다.   ‘오퍼레이션 캡슐 볼트’로 명명한 이 사이버 공격은, 지난달 서울에서 열린 ‘왜 지금 원산갈마 관광인가?’라는 제목의 실제 학술행사를 공격에 활용했다.  news1.kr/nk/politics-diploma…  위 학술 컨퍼런스에서 언급한 원산 갈마 관광지구는, 김정은이 외화벌이를 위해 공을 들여온 곳이다. youtu.be/fOsaF-f6P9E?si=-ksn…  위 컨퍼런스가 열린 지 열흘 뒤, 해킹조직은 통일 관련 업체를 사칭하여, 연구, 정책, 학술 분야 종사자들에게 행사 자료집을 보내는 것처럼 꾸민 이메일을 발송했다.  이메일에는 행사 자료집을 내려받을 수 있는 것처럼 보이는 링크가 포함됐지만, 이용자가 해당 링크를 통해 파일을 내려받아 실행하면 악성코드가 컴퓨터에 설치되는 방식이었다.   사이버 보안 기관들은 APT37이 적어도 2012년부터 활동해 왔으며,  주로 한국 내 정부와 군 관련 기관, 탈북민 등을 표적으로 삼아 공격해 온 것으로 분석했다.
영국 정부가 13일 영국 내 언론인 암살 모의와 사이버 공격 등에 연루된 이란 혁명수비대(IRGC)를 국가안보 위협 단체로 지정했습니다.  더 자세한 기사  👉voakorea.com/a/uk-to-ban-ira… voakorea.com/a/uk-to-ban-ira…

테러조직의 AI 챗봇 악용이 선전·번역을 넘어 작전 지원 영역으로 확대되고 있다. 최근 케임브리지대 연구에서는 보코하람 전 조직원들의 복수 증언을 통해 AI 챗봇이 폭발물 제작 정보 탐색, 무기 고장 해결, 공격 계획과 전투영상 분석 등에 활용된 정황이 보고. 다만 연구가 주로 인터뷰에 기반한 만큼, 이를 독립적으로 검증된 복수의 테러 사건으로 단정하기보다는 조직적 활용 가능성을 보여주는 현장 증거로 해석할 필요  Anthropic은 Claude Fable 5를 Pro·Max·Team 등 유료 플랜 이용자가 기존 주간 한도의 일정 범위에서 추가 요금 없이 사용할 수 있는 기간을 미국 태평양시간 기준 7월 19일까지 연장. 이는 일반 무료 이용자에 대한 전면 개방은 아니지만, 고성능 모델의 이용자와 사용량이 확대될수록 안전장치 우회 시도도 증가할 수 있다는 접근성·보안 간 딜레마를 부각  한편 인도는 중요 인프라 방어 역량 확보를 위해 미국 및 관련 기업과 Anthropic의 고성능 사이버 모델 Mythos에 대한 접근 방안을 협의 중인 것으로 보도. 단기적으로는 Fable 5 프로모션 종료 이후의 과금·접근 정책, Mythos와 같은 고위험 모델의 국가별 허용 기준, 그리고 각국이 테러 특화 안전성 평가와 모델 접근통제를 얼마나 구체적인 제도로 전환하는지가 주요 관전 포인트  jcplanetinvestment.com #Anthropic #JCPR
네덜란드 외무부, 사이버 공격 연루 의혹으로 러시아 대사 소환 (출처 : Russia.kr.. | blog.naver.com/paxeurasia/22… 블로그) naver.me/xM5uM2lL

[Citi] 美, 중국 AI 추격 속 오픈소스 규제 고심…데이터센터 전력 전망도 재검토  트럼프 행정부와 미국 인공지능 업계가 중국의 선도적 오픈소스 AI 모델을 기준으로 미국산 모델의 출시 허용 범위를 정하는 새로운 역량 평가 체계를 논의하고 있습니다.  중국산 고성능 오픈소스 모델의 확산을 막기 어려운 상황에서, 미국도 경쟁력 있는 오픈소스 생태계를 육성해야 한다는 현실론과 사이버·생물학적 위험을 통제해야 한다는 안보론이 정면으로 충돌하는 모습입니다.  ——————————————————  중국 모델 수준 이하면 출시 절차 간소화 검토  워싱턴포스트 산하 WP 인텔리전스에 따르면, 트럼프 행정부와 AI 업계 단체들은 미국산 AI 모델의 성능이 중국의 주요 오픈소스 모델과 같거나 낮을 경우 시장 출시 절차를 간소화하는 방안을 논의해 왔습니다.  적용 대상은 완전히 공개되는 오픈소스 모델뿐 아니라 기업이 사용권을 부여하는 비공개 라이선스 모델까지 포함될 수 있습니다.  현재 논의는 별도의 오픈소스 AI 행정명령을 새로 만드는 것보다는, 트럼프 행정부가 지난 6월 발표한 기존 AI 행정명령의 적용 기준을 구체화하는 방향에 초점이 맞춰져 있습니다.  이 같은 방안이 도입되면 미국 상무부 산하 AI 표준혁신센터가 미국 모델과 중국 모델의 성능을 공식적으로 비교하는 평가 절차를 마련해야 할 가능성이 큽니다.  ——————————————————  중국산 오픈소스 모델, ‘트로이 목마’ 우려  미국 정책 당국이 가장 우려하는 부분은 중국산 오픈소스 모델이 단순한 저가형 AI 도구를 넘어 국가안보 위협으로 활용될 가능성입니다.  일부 전문가들은 중국산 모델에 악성 소프트웨어가 숨어 있거나, 중국 당국이 악용할 수 있는 백도어가 의도적으로 삽입됐을 가능성을 제기하고 있습니다.  신미국안보센터의 대니얼 렘러 선임연구원은 중국 모델에서 적대적 공격에 취약한 심각한 보안 결함이 발견됐으며, 이러한 취약점이 정보 수집이나 사이버 공격에 악용될 수 있다고 지적했습니다.  반면 오픈소스 지지자들은 중국 모델이 저렴하고 접근성이 높으며 수정도 쉬운 만큼, 전 세계 기업의 사용 확대를 막기 어렵다고 주장합니다.  중국산 모델의 확산을 억제하기 어렵다면 미국도 기업들이 안심하고 활용할 수 있는 경쟁력 있는 오픈소스 대안을 제공해야 한다는 논리입니다.  ——————————————————  한 번 공개되면 회수 불가능한 오픈소스의 딜레마  오픈소스 모델의 가장 큰 문제는 한 번 인터넷에 공개되면 사실상 회수가 불가능하다는 점입니다.  비공개 모델은 정부가 기업에 서비스 중단이나 수정, 배포 철회를 요구할 수 있지만, 오픈소스 모델은 가중치와 구조가 공개되는 순간 전 세계로 복제될 수 있습니다.  기업이 모델에 안전장치를 학습시키더라도 사용자가 이를 제거하거나 재학습할 수 있으며, 실제 사용 방식도 추적하기 어렵습니다.  헬렌 토너 조지타운대 안보·신흥기술센터 전무이사는 오픈소스 모델의 안전장치는 다시 제거하기 쉬운 만큼, 비공개 모델과는 다른 위험 기준을 적용해야 한다고 강조했습니다.  ——————————————————  6~12개월 내 ‘미토스급’ 모델 무료 배포 가능성  AI 업계는 향후 6개월에서 12개월 안에 고도의 사이버 공격 능력을 갖춘 중국산 ‘미토스급’ 모델이 인터넷에서 무료로 배포될 수 있다고 보고 있습니다.  중국 모델이 현재 오픈AI와 앤스로픽의 최첨단 모델보다 약 6개월 뒤처져 있다는 분석을 감안하면, 성능 격차가 빠르게 좁혀질 수 있다는 판단입니다.  머신러닝 엔지니어 네이선 램버트는 GPT-5.5, 클로드 오퍼스 4.8, GLM-5.2 수준을 의미 있게 넘어서는 오픈웨이트 모델의 출시가 금지되거나 무기한 연기될 가능성이 있다고 전망했습니다.  앤스로픽의 다리오 아모데이 최고경영자도 중국 기업들이 미국의 선도 모델 성능을 복제하기 위해 반복적으로 ‘증류 공격’을 시도하고 있다며 경고해 왔습니다.  아모데이는 수개월 뒤에는 미토스급 사이버 역량을 갖춘 모델을 누구나 내려받을 수 있게 될 수 있다고 우려했습니다.  ——————————————————  전면 금지 현실성은 낮아  미국 정부와 의회가 중국산 오픈소스 모델의 사용을 전면 금지하는 방안도 거론되고 있지만, 실효성에는 의문이 제기됩니다.  오픈소스 모델은 일단 공개되면 파일 공유 사이트나 해외 서버를 통해 복제·재배포될 수 있기 때문에 완전한 차단이 어렵습니다.  공화당의 한 AI 정책 담당 보좌관도 중국산 모델을 실제로 금지하기는 쉽지 않겠지만, 의회가 관련 조치를 시도할 가능성은 있다고 평가했습니다.  결국 미국은 중국산 모델의 확산을 막기 어려운 상황에서 자국 오픈소스 산업을 육성해야 하는 동시에, 위험한 고성능 모델의 공개를 어디까지 허용할지 결정해야 하는 딜레마에 놓였습니다.  ——————————————————  데이터센터 전력 수요, 장기 전망 과도할 수도  한편 AI 데이터센터의 전력 소비량이 장기적으로 현재 전망보다 적을 수 있다는 분석도 제기됐습니다.  현재 데이터센터 산업의 전력 수요 전망은 전기는 구리를 통해 흐르고, 데이터는 전기 신호로 이동하며, 대부분의 AI 연산은 대형 클라우드 데이터센터에서 이뤄진다는 세 가지 전제를 기반으로 합니다.  그러나 초전도체와 포토닉스, 온디바이스 AI 기술이 발전하면서 이 같은 전제가 동시에 흔들리고 있습니다.  ——————————————————  구리 대신 초전도체, 전기 신호 대신 빛  기업들은 기존 구리 배선보다 전력 손실이 적은 고온 초전도체를 데이터센터에 적용하는 방안을 검토하고 있습니다.  고온 초전도체는 전기 저항을 거의 제거할 수 있지만, 특수 냉각 설비 비용이 높아 그동안 상용화에 한계가 있었습니다.  다만 AI 데이터센터의 전력 수요가 급증하면서 초기 비용을 감수하더라도 장기적인 에너지 절감 효과가 더 커질 수 있다는 분석이 나오고 있습니다.  데이터 전송 방식에서도 전기 신호 대신 빛을 활용하는 포토닉스 기술이 주목받고 있습니다.  라이트매터 등 관련 기업들은 빛을 이용하면 데이터 전송 속도를 높이는 동시에 반도체 냉각에 필요한 전력도 줄일 수 있다고 주장합니다.  ——————————————————  AI 연산, 클라우드에서 기기로 이동  애플과 퀄컴, 엔비디아를 비롯한 주요 기업들은 모든 AI 요청을 초대형 데이터센터로 보내는 대신 스마트폰과 개인용 기기에서 직접 처리하는 로컬 AI 기술을 개발하고 있습니다.  이 같은 온디바이스 AI가 확산되면 데이터센터로 집중되는 연산 부하와 전력 수요가 시장 예상보다 낮아질 수 있습니다.  과거 산성비 규제나 인터넷 트래픽 급증 당시에도 초기에는 막대한 인프라 투자가 필요할 것으로 예상됐지만, 배출 제어 기술과 광섬유 혁신으로 실제 비용과 설비 수요는 전망을 크게 밑돌았습니다.  WP 인텔리전스는 AI 전력 수요 역시 기술 혁신에 따라 예상보다 완만하게 증가할 수 있다며, 기업들이 장기 자본투자를 여러 기술 시나리오에 대입해 점검해야 한다고 조언했습니다.  ——————————————————  오픈소스 규제와 전력 투자 모두 ‘기술 변화’가 변수  이번 분석의 핵심은 AI 산업의 두 가지 장기 전제가 동시에 시험대에 올랐다는 점입니다.  정책 측면에서는 중국 모델을 막는 것만으로 미국의 AI 경쟁력을 지키기 어렵고, 산업 측면에서는 현재의 데이터센터 전력 수요 전망이 기술 혁신을 충분히 반영하지 못했을 가능성이 있습니다.  향후 미국 AI 정책은 위험한 모델의 공개를 통제하면서도 중국에 뒤처지지 않는 오픈소스 생태계를 구축할 수 있느냐에 달려 있습니다.  데이터센터 산업 역시 AI 연산량 증가가 전력 수요 증가로 그대로 이어질 것이라는 기존 가정에서 벗어나, 초전도체와 포토닉스, 로컬 AI의 확산 속도를 함께 살펴봐야 할 것으로 보입니다.
